mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of initial setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are required for Cluster API to function properly, as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for version 1.12.4.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller in the specified namespace, enabling it to manage clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version 1.12.4

```

> This installs the Cluster API controller with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource that defines a new Kubernetes cluster managed by Cluster API.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your specific infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API controller pods running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will lead to data loss for any clusters managed by Cluster API.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API controller.
> This command removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster resources. After upgrading, verify that the components are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> Backup existing cluster resources.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API controller.
> Verify that the Cluster API pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not available.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Cluster API controller is not responding or is unhealthy.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Cluster API controller pod using: kubectl logs -l app=cluster-api -n capi-system

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider is installed and configured properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <ip>:<port>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of your Kubernetes cluster and ensure that the API server is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version for Cluster resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your Cluster resource definitions to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your clusters with their statuses, confirming that Cluster API is functioning correctly.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and tools installed.
